content.claim: Since 2013 KLIK centralization, decision-making authority shifted toward central government while pedagogical decisions remain with schools, creating tension and failing to solve structural funding challenges
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Assessment of dysfunctionality is based on commentary rather than quantitative reform outcome measures
Causal attribution to centralization specifically versus other concurrent policy changes is not isolated
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
